properties.text: For any given set of key-value pairs in trie._data, the root() function must always produce the same 32-byte Root value, regardless of the order in which keys were inserted via trie_set.

reachability.notes: An attacker submitting transactions via P2P controls the order and content of state modifications. If root computation is non-deterministic, different nodes would compute different state roots for the same block, causing a consensus split.

properties.severity_justification: Non-deterministic state root computation would cause consensus failure across the entire Ethereum network. All validators would disagree on the canonical chain, leading to a network-wide split.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate two valid orderings of the same state changes producing different state roots.

properties.text: The patricialize function must terminate for any valid input. Specifically, for every recursive call, the level parameter must strictly increase (level_recursive > level_caller), guaranteeing termination within at most max_nibble_length iterations.

reachability.notes: An attacker controls storage keys and account addresses via transactions. If patricialize can be made to recurse infinitely, block processing would hang (DoS). Secured tries hash keys with keccak256, bounding depth to 64 nibbles, but the property must hold for all trie modes.

properties.severity_justification: Infinite recursion during state root computation would cause all nodes processing the block to crash (stack overflow) or hang indefinitely, halting the network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a set of key-value pairs that causes patricialize to exceed expected recursion depth or fail to terminate.

properties.text: For all keys k in trie._data: trie._data[k] != trie.default. Equivalently, trie_set(trie, key, trie.default) must result in key being absent from trie._data.

reachability.notes: Transactions cause state writes via EVM which invoke trie_set. If a default-valued key persists in _data (ghost entry), it would be included in the Merkle tree, producing an incorrect state root and consensus divergence.

properties.severity_justification: Ghost entries in trie._data would produce incorrect state roots. Different implementations handling this differently would cause consensus splits. Could also be exploited for state bloat attacks.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must show a sequence of trie_set operations that leaves a default-valued key in _data, producing a different root than expected.

properties.text: trie_set(trie, key, value) followed by trie_get(trie, key) must return value. trie_get on a key never set must return trie.default.

reachability.notes: Every state read/write during transaction execution flows through trie_get/trie_set. A violation means the EVM reads incorrect state, leading to wrong execution results and consensus failure.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect state reads would cause the EVM to produce wrong execution results for every transaction touching the affected key. This breaks consensus and could lead to fund loss.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a key-value pair where trie_get returns a value different from what was set via trie_set.

properties.text: When trie.secured is True, all keys stored in the prepared trie mapping (output of _prepare_trie) must be bytes_to_nibble_list(keccak256(preimage)) for their original preimage. No raw (unhashed) keys may appear in the mapping of a secured trie.

reachability.notes: If secured trie key hashing is bypassed, an attacker could choose key positions in the trie, enabling trie-balancing attacks that cause deep nesting and DoS during root computation or proof verification.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing key hashing in secured tries would enable an attacker to craft pathological trie structures, causing DoS during state root computation. Could make block processing extremely slow or cause timeouts.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must show a code path where a secured trie processes unhashed keys, or demonstrate a trie-balancing attack exploiting this.

properties.text: For all values v produced by encode_node: v != b''. The _prepare_trie function must assert this for every encoded value before including it in the output mapping.

reachability.notes: An attacker's transaction could create a state value that, when passed through encode_node, produces empty bytes. This would either trigger an assertion failure (crashing the node) or, if the assertion is missing, corrupt the trie structure.

properties.severity_justification: An empty encoded value either crashes the node (assertion failure during block processing) or silently corrupts the state trie if the assertion is bypassed. Both outcomes are severe.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a value type or edge case in encode_node that produces empty bytes.

properties.text: When encoding an Account node via encode_node, the get_storage_root callback must be invoked and must return a non-None value. encode_node(account, storage_root=None) must not silently succeed.

reachability.notes: Transactions create or modify accounts. If the state module fails to provide a storage root callback, or the callback returns None for a newly created account, the encoding would fail or produce an incorrect account encoding.

properties.severity_justification: A missing or null storage root would either crash the node during block processing (assertion failure) or produce an incorrect account encoding, corrupting the state root and causing consensus failure.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a transaction that creates a state where get_storage_root returns None for an Account node.

properties.text: The get_storage_root callback must return the correct, canonical 32-byte storage root for the given address. If the callback returns an incorrect root, the state root will be silently corrupted.

reachability.notes: The trie embeds the storage root directly without validation beyond the None check. An incorrect storage root from the callback silently propagates into the state root. The attacker controls which accounts are modified, potentially triggering edge cases in storage root computation.

properties.severity_justification: Silent corruption of the state root via incorrect storage roots would cause consensus splits. The trie has no mechanism to detect or prevent this, making it a critical trust boundary violation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a transaction or state configuration that causes get_storage_root to return an incorrect value.

properties.text: encode_internal_node must accept only nodes of type None, LeafNode, ExtensionNode, or BranchNode. Any other type must cause an AssertionError. No silent fallthrough is permitted.

reachability.notes: encode_internal_node receives its input from patricialize, which only produces valid node types. An external attacker cannot directly supply an invalid node type to encode_internal_node.

properties.severity_justification: While type confusion in encode_internal_node would cause incorrect encoding, the function only receives input from patricialize which constrains output types. Exploitation requires an internal bug in patricialize.

properties.text: For encode_internal_node: if len(rlp.encode(unencoded)) < 32, the function returns the unencoded form. If len(rlp.encode(unencoded)) >= 32, the function returns keccak256(rlp.encode(unencoded)). The threshold is exactly 32 bytes with no off-by-one error.

reachability.notes: An attacker can craft state values that produce encoded nodes near the 32-byte boundary. An off-by-one error in the threshold check would cause some nodes to be hashed when they should be inlined (or vice versa), producing different trie structures across implementations.

properties.severity_justification: An off-by-one in the 32-byte threshold would cause different trie structures for nodes near the boundary. This creates a consensus split between implementations that handle the boundary differently.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a node encoding that is exactly 32 bytes and show inconsistent handling across the boundary.

properties.text: RLP encoding must be canonical and deterministic: for any input x, rlp.encode(x) always produces the same byte sequence. Two different calls with structurally identical inputs must produce bit-identical output.

reachability.notes: Non-canonical RLP encoding would cause different state roots across implementations or even across different runs of the same implementation. An attacker could trigger edge cases in RLP encoding via crafted transactions.

properties.severity_justification: Non-deterministic or non-canonical RLP encoding would break consensus. The 32-byte threshold in encode_internal_node depends on exact encoded length, making even small encoding differences catastrophic.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate an input that produces non-canonical or inconsistent RLP encoding.

properties.text: keccak256 must produce consistent, deterministic 32-byte output for any input. For identical inputs, keccak256(x) == keccak256(x) must always hold. The function must be collision-resistant.

reachability.notes: keccak256 is used in three critical paths: key hashing, node hashing, and root computation. Inconsistency in any path breaks consensus. A supply-chain attack on the crypto library would affect all paths simultaneously.

properties.severity_justification: keccak256 is the fundamental cryptographic primitive for the entire trie. Inconsistency would break all secured trie lookups, all node hashing, and all root computations simultaneously.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate inconsistent keccak256 output or a practical collision.

properties.text: In secured tries, if keccak256(addr_a) == keccak256(addr_b) for addr_a != addr_b, then the trie would overwrite one account's state with another's. This must not be practically achievable (collision resistance).

reachability.notes: An attacker who can find keccak256 collisions could craft two addresses that map to the same trie key, causing one account to overwrite another. This would allow theft of funds.

properties.severity_justification: A keccak256 collision would allow an attacker to overwrite any account's state, enabling direct theft of funds. This is the most catastrophic possible attack on the trie.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a practical keccak256 collision for Ethereum addresses. Currently considered infeasible given 128-bit collision resistance.

properties.text: encode_node must correctly dispatch to the appropriate encoding logic based on type: Account -> encode_account with storage_root, LegacyTransaction/Receipt/Withdrawal/U256 -> rlp.encode, Bytes -> identity, otherwise -> previous_trie.encode_node. No type must be silently misclassified.

reachability.notes: If a new type is introduced that is not handled by the current fork's isinstance chain, it falls through to previous_trie.encode_node. An attacker creating contracts or state transitions that produce unexpected types could trigger misclassification.

properties.severity_justification: Type misclassification in encode_node would produce incorrect encodings for the affected value type. If the wrong encoding is used, the state root would differ from the canonical value, causing consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a value type that is incorrectly dispatched by encode_node's isinstance chain.

properties.text: The fallback to previous_trie.encode_node for unrecognized types must produce output that is semantically compatible with the current fork's trie structure. The output must be non-empty bytes and must be a valid RLP-encodable or hashable value.

reachability.notes: If the previous fork's encoder has a bug or produces non-canonical output for a type that falls through, the current fork would silently incorporate the incorrect encoding into its state root.

properties.severity_justification: A bug in the previous fork's encoder that is triggered via the fallback path would silently corrupt the current fork's state root. Cross-fork encoding inconsistencies are particularly dangerous because they may not be caught by same-fork tests.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a type that falls through to previous_trie.encode_node and produces incorrect or non-canonical encoding.

properties.text: copy_trie must produce a new Trie object that is independent from the original. Modifications to the copy's _data must not affect the original's _data, and vice versa.

reachability.notes: Trie copying is used for state snapshots during transaction execution (for revert on failure). If copy_trie produces a shallow copy that shares mutable state, reverting a failed transaction would not restore the original state, or modifications during speculative execution would leak into the canonical state.

properties.severity_justification: If copy_trie fails to isolate the copy from the original, state reverts during failed transactions would not work correctly. An attacker could craft transactions that fail but leave state modifications behind, potentially creating or destroying value.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a sequence of operations where copy_trie fails to isolate the copy, causing state corruption on revert.

properties.text: copy_trie's shallow copy is safe only because trie values are frozen (immutable) objects. If a mutable value is stored in the trie, copy_trie must still produce an independent copy (or the trie must reject mutable values).

reachability.notes: Exploitation requires internal code changes that store mutable objects in the trie. External attackers cannot control the mutability of trie values.

properties.severity_justification: If future code changes introduce mutable values into the trie, copy_trie's shallow copy would cause aliasing bugs. This is a correctness property that guards against future regressions.

properties.text: trie_set(trie, key, trie.default) when key is already in trie._data must delete the key from _data. The resulting trie must not contain the key.

reachability.notes: An attacker's transaction can cause storage slot deletions (setting to zero) which invoke trie_set with the default value. If deletion fails, the ghost entry persists in the trie, inflating the state root and potentially causing consensus divergence.

properties.severity_justification: Failed deletion would cause state bloat (non-reclaimable entries) and produce incorrect state roots. This directly impacts consensus and could be exploited for state-size DoS.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a storage deletion that leaves a ghost entry in _data.

properties.text: trie_set(trie, key, trie.default) when key is NOT in trie._data must be a no-op. The trie must not be modified.

reachability.notes: An attacker's transaction could attempt to delete a non-existent key. If this erroneously modifies _data (e.g., inserting a default-valued entry), it would corrupt the trie.

properties.severity_justification: While unlikely to cause immediate harm, inserting a default-valued entry could corrupt the state root calculation for subsequent operations.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate that deleting a non-existent key modifies the trie state.

properties.text: trie_get(trie, key) must return trie.default for any key not in trie._data. The return value must be exactly trie.default, not a copy or equivalent value.

reachability.notes: Every state read during EVM execution flows through trie_get. If a missing key returns something other than the default, the EVM would execute with incorrect state, producing wrong results.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect default returns would cause the EVM to misinterpret state for non-existent accounts or storage slots, potentially leading to incorrect balance calculations or contract behavior.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate trie_get returning a non-default value for a key that was never set.

properties.text: common_prefix_length(a, b) must return a value in the range [0, min(len(a), len(b))]. The return value must equal the exact number of leading elements shared between a and b.

reachability.notes: common_prefix_length is called by patricialize with nibble-list keys. The inputs are derived from keccak256 hashes (in secured tries). While an attacker controls the original keys, the hashed nibble lists are effectively random.

properties.severity_justification: An incorrect common_prefix_length would cause patricialize to create wrong trie structures (incorrect extension nodes or branch nodes), leading to wrong state roots.

properties.text: For all j < common_prefix_length(a, b): a[j] == b[j]. The prefix identified by the function must be a genuine common prefix.

reachability.notes: Pure algorithmic correctness property. Inputs are internal nibble lists.

properties.severity_justification: If the common prefix is incorrectly identified, extension nodes in the trie would have wrong key segments, corrupting the trie structure and producing incorrect state roots.

properties.text: All nibble values in the input to nibble_list_to_compact must be in range [0, 15]. If any nibble value is outside this range, the compact encoding is incorrect.

reachability.notes: Nibble lists are produced by bytes_to_nibble_list which guarantees values in [0,15] via bit masking. However, if a code path bypasses bytes_to_nibble_list, invalid nibbles could reach nibble_list_to_compact.

properties.severity_justification: Invalid nibble values would produce incorrect compact encodings, corrupting trie node paths. However, bytes_to_nibble_list's bit masking normally prevents this.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a code path where nibble values outside [0,15] reach nibble_list_to_compact.

properties.text: nibble_list_to_compact must correctly encode the is_leaf flag and parity in the high nibble of the first byte: even-length leaf -> 0x20, even-length extension -> 0x00, odd-length leaf -> 0x3X, odd-length extension -> 0x1X (where X is first nibble).

reachability.notes: The compact encoding header distinguishes leaf nodes from extension nodes in the trie. An incorrect header would cause proof verification to fail or interpret nodes as the wrong type, breaking Merkle proofs.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect compact encoding headers would produce invalid Merkle proofs and incorrect state roots. This breaks consensus and makes state proofs unverifiable by light clients.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a leaf/extension node whose compact encoding has an incorrect header byte.

properties.text: The output length of nibble_list_to_compact must be exactly ceil((len(input) + 1) / 2) bytes.

reachability.notes: Output length correctness is a pure algorithmic property. Incorrect length would produce malformed compact encodings affecting RLP encoding and the 32-byte threshold.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect output length would produce malformed compact encodings, potentially affecting the RLP encoding size and the 32-byte threshold decision in encode_internal_node.

properties.text: bytes_to_nibble_list output length must be exactly 2 * len(input). Each output nibble must be in range [0, 15].

reachability.notes: Pure algorithmic property of the nibble conversion function. The bit-masking operations guarantee nibble range correctness.

properties.severity_justification: The bit-masking operations (& 0xF0, >> 4, & 0x0F) mathematically guarantee the output range. A violation would require a fundamental platform bug.

properties.text: bytes_to_nibble_list and nibble_list_to_compact must be inverses (modulo the compact header byte). For any byte sequence b: nibble_list_to_compact(bytes_to_nibble_list(b), is_leaf) must produce a compact encoding that, when decoded, yields b.

reachability.notes: Roundtrip property of the nibble conversion functions. A violation would cause trie keys to be corrupted during encoding/decoding, producing incorrect trie structures.

properties.severity_justification: Loss of roundtrip correctness would corrupt trie key paths, producing incorrect trie structures and state roots.

properties.text: patricialize({}) must return None, which encode_internal_node maps to b''. An empty trie must produce the canonical EMPTY_TRIE_ROOT = keccak256(rlp.encode(b'')).

reachability.notes: An attacker could create a transaction that empties all storage for a contract. If the empty storage trie does not produce EMPTY_TRIE_ROOT, the account's state root would be incorrect, causing consensus divergence.

properties.severity_justification: An incorrect empty trie root would affect every account with no storage, which includes newly created accounts and accounts with cleared storage. This would cause widespread consensus failure.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate that an empty trie produces a root different from EMPTY_TRIE_ROOT.

properties.text: patricialize with a single-element input must always return a LeafNode containing the key suffix (from level onward) and the value.

reachability.notes: A trie with a single entry (e.g., a contract with one storage slot) would trigger this path. Incorrect handling would produce a wrong trie structure.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect single-element trie construction would produce wrong state roots for all single-entry sub-tries in the state trie, affecting contracts with limited storage.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a single-element trie that produces an incorrect root.

properties.text: In patricialize, when keys share a common prefix, an ExtensionNode must be created. The ExtensionNode's key_segment must exactly match the shared prefix, and the subnode must be the result of recursively patricializing with level increased by the prefix length.

reachability.notes: In secured tries, keccak256-hashed keys may share common prefixes by chance. Incorrect extension node construction would produce wrong trie structures for those key groups.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect extension node construction would produce wrong trie structures and state roots for any set of keys sharing a prefix. This affects Merkle proofs and consensus.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate key sets where the extension node is incorrectly constructed.

properties.text: In patricialize, when keys have no shared prefix at the current level, a BranchNode must be created with exactly 16 subnodes (one per nibble value 0-15) and a value field. Each subnode must contain only keys whose nibble at 'level' matches the branch index.

reachability.notes: Branch nodes are the primary branching structure. Incorrect key distribution among branches would produce wrong trie structures and corrupt all paths through the branch.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect branch node construction would misplace keys in the trie, making them unretrievable via correct paths. This breaks state access and Merkle proofs.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a branch node with incorrectly distributed keys.

properties.text: Account, Receipt, and Uint values must never appear in a BranchNode's value position (where len(key) == level). If such a value appears at this position, patricialize must raise AssertionError.

reachability.notes: In secured tries, all keys are keccak256 hashes (32 bytes = 64 nibbles), so they all have the same length. The level can only equal key length if the trie has a single entry. For Account/Receipt/Uint types to appear at this position would require an unusual state configuration.

properties.severity_justification: If this assertion fails during block processing, the node crashes. If the assertion is missing or bypassed, the Account/Receipt/Uint value would be placed in an invalid position, corrupting the trie structure.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a state configuration where an Account/Receipt/Uint appears at a BranchNode value position.

properties.text: The root() function must always return a value of exactly 32 bytes. Whether the root_node's RLP encoding is < 32 bytes or >= 32 bytes, the final return value must be a 32-byte Root.

reachability.notes: The state root is included in block headers and verified by all nodes. A non-32-byte root would cause header validation failures or be misinterpreted, leading to consensus issues.

properties.severity_justification: A non-32-byte root would break block header format, causing all nodes to reject the block or interpret the header incorrectly. This is a fundamental protocol violation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a trie state that causes root() to return a value that is not exactly 32 bytes.

properties.text: An attacker must not be able to craft transaction inputs (addresses, storage keys) that cause the patricialize function to create pathologically deep or unbalanced trie structures in unsecured tries, resulting in excessive computation during root() computation.

reachability.notes: In secured tries, keccak256 key hashing randomizes key distribution, preventing deliberate trie-balancing attacks. However, if an unsecured trie is used (e.g., for transactions or receipts), an attacker can choose keys to create deeply nested structures.

properties.severity_justification: Pathological trie structures in unsecured tries could cause excessive computation during root calculation, potentially causing block processing timeouts and DoS.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate crafted keys that create pathologically deep trie structures causing measurable performance degradation in root computation.

properties.text: The encode_node function must not silently encode a new/unrecognized type via the previous_trie fallback when the current fork should handle it explicitly. Type dispatch must follow a strict, deterministic isinstance chain.

reachability.notes: If a new type is introduced in a fork upgrade and not added to the isinstance chain, it would silently fall through to the previous fork's encoder, which may encode it differently or incorrectly.

properties.severity_justification: Silent type fallthrough across forks could produce incorrect encodings that differ between implementations, causing consensus splits after fork upgrades.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a type that falls through the isinstance chain and is incorrectly encoded by the previous fork's encoder.

properties.text: The get_storage_root callback must not be substitutable by external actors. The callback reference must be constructed within the trusted state module and passed directly to _prepare_trie/root without exposure to external API surfaces.

reachability.notes: The callback is constructed within the trusted state module. There is no external API for supplying an arbitrary callback. Exploitation would require code injection or internal module compromise.

properties.severity_justification: If an attacker could substitute the storage root callback, they could inject arbitrary storage roots into any account, effectively spoofing the entire storage state. However, this requires internal access.

properties.text: Direct manipulation of trie._data (bypassing trie_set) must not be possible through any external-facing API. All state modifications must go through trie_set to maintain the default-value deletion invariant.

reachability.notes: Python does not enforce access control on _data. The underscore prefix is a convention only. However, no external API exposes _data directly. Exploitation requires internal code that bypasses trie_set.

properties.severity_justification: Direct _data manipulation could insert values violating the default-value invariant, creating ghost entries that corrupt the Merkle root. However, this requires internal access.

properties.text: An attacker must not be able to cause the total number of state entries (accounts + storage slots) to grow unboundedly within a single block, causing root() computation to exceed block processing time limits.

reachability.notes: Gas costs for SSTORE and CREATE limit state growth per transaction, and the block gas limit caps total gas per block. However, if gas costs are miscalibrated, an attacker could create more state entries than expected for the gas spent.

properties.severity_justification: Unbounded state growth per block would cause root computation to take longer than the block interval, making it impossible for honest validators to keep up. This would effectively halt the network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a transaction or block that creates significantly more state entries than the gas cost model predicts, causing measurable root computation slowdown.

properties.text: The encode_internal_node function, when called with None, must return b'' (empty bytes). This represents an absent node in the trie and must be handled consistently across all call sites.

reachability.notes: None input to encode_internal_node represents an empty branch in the trie. This is a normal internal condition, not attacker-reachable.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect handling of None in encode_internal_node would produce wrong BranchNode encodings for empty branches. This is a correctness issue that would be caught by basic tests.

properties.text: The root() function's handling of small root nodes must be correct: when the root_node's RLP encoding is < 32 bytes, the function must hash it with keccak256 to produce a 32-byte root. It must NOT return the unencoded form directly as the state root.

reachability.notes: A trie with very few entries could produce a root node whose RLP encoding is < 32 bytes. If the function returns the unencoded form instead of hashing it, the state root would be shorter than 32 bytes, breaking the block header format.

properties.severity_justification: Returning a non-32-byte state root would break block header validation across all nodes. This is a protocol-level failure that would halt consensus.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a trie with few entries where root() returns a value shorter than 32 bytes.

properties.text: The _prepare_trie function must process ALL entries in trie._data. No entry may be skipped or omitted from the output mapping. The number of entries in the output mapping must equal the number of entries in trie._data.

reachability.notes: If _prepare_trie skips an entry (e.g., due to an exception in encode_node that is silently caught), the state root would not include that entry's data, causing a consensus divergence.

properties.severity_justification: Skipping a trie entry would produce an incorrect state root that omits valid state. This could cause accounts or storage to disappear from the canonical state, leading to fund loss.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a state entry that is silently skipped by _prepare_trie.

properties.text: In secured tries, _prepare_trie must not have key collisions in the output mapping. If two different preimages hash to the same keccak256 value (collision), the second entry would overwrite the first, losing state data.

reachability.notes: This property relies on keccak256 collision resistance. A practical collision would allow an attacker to create two addresses/keys that map to the same trie key, causing state overwrites.

properties.severity_justification: Key collisions would cause one account's state to overwrite another's, enabling theft of funds. This is the most direct path to financial exploitation in the trie.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a practical keccak256 collision in the context of Ethereum addresses or storage keys.

properties.text: The size-dependent information boundary in encode_internal_node (returning unencoded form for < 32 bytes, hash for >= 32 bytes) must not leak information that enables attacks. Specifically, the decision boundary must be consistent and must not allow an attacker to determine node sizes beyond what is publicly available.

reachability.notes: Ethereum state is public by design. The 32-byte threshold is a space optimization, not a privacy mechanism. Information disclosure through trie structure is by design.

properties.severity_justification: This is by design in the Merkle Patricia Trie specification. Ethereum state is public. The threshold exposes node sizes but this is intentional, not a vulnerability.

properties.text: The iteration order of trie._data.items() in _prepare_trie must not affect the final root. The patricialize function must produce a canonical tree structure regardless of iteration order.

reachability.notes: Different Python dict implementations or versions could iterate in different orders. If patricialize is order-dependent, different implementations would compute different roots. The Merkle Patricia Trie is designed to be order-independent.

properties.severity_justification: Order-dependent root computation would cause consensus splits between implementations using different dict iteration orders. This is a fundamental correctness requirement.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate two orderings of the same key-value pairs producing different roots.

properties.text: When encode_node receives a Bytes value, it must return the value unchanged (identity encoding). No transformation, re-encoding, or validation must be applied to Bytes values.

reachability.notes: Bytes values in the trie represent already-encoded data (e.g., contract bytecode). Any transformation would corrupt the encoding.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect handling of Bytes values would corrupt already-encoded data, but this is a straightforward type check that is unlikely to fail.

properties.text: The _prepare_trie function must correctly identify Account values and route them through encode_node with the storage root from get_storage_root. Non-Account values must be routed through encode_node without a storage root.

reachability.notes: If a non-Account value is incorrectly identified as an Account, _prepare_trie would call get_storage_root for it, potentially crashing or producing an incorrect encoding. Conversely, if an Account is not identified, it would be encoded without a storage root.

properties.severity_justification: Misidentifying Account vs non-Account values would produce incorrect encodings: Accounts without storage roots or non-Accounts with spurious storage roots. Both corrupt the state root.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a value type that is misclassified by the isinstance(value, Account) check in _prepare_trie.

properties.text: For any trie state S, root(S) must produce a value equal to the Merkle root computed by any other correct implementation of the Ethereum Merkle Patricia Trie specification for the same logical state S.

reachability.notes: This is the ultimate consensus property. If this implementation produces a different root than other correct implementations for the same state, the network splits.

properties.severity_justification: Cross-implementation divergence is the definition of a consensus failure. Any state that produces different roots across implementations constitutes a critical bug.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a specific state where this implementation's root() produces a different value than another correct implementation.

properties.text: The LeafNode's rest_of_key in patricialize must be the key suffix starting from 'level'. It must contain exactly key[level:] nibbles. An off-by-one in the slice would corrupt the trie path.

reachability.notes: An off-by-one in the LeafNode key suffix would make the key unresolvable via Merkle proofs, effectively making the state entry inaccessible.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect LeafNode key suffixes would produce wrong Merkle proofs and make state entries inaccessible. This breaks state access and light client verification.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate a LeafNode with an incorrect rest_of_key causing proof verification failure.

properties.text: The BranchNode created by patricialize must always have exactly 16 subnodes (indices 0-15), regardless of how many are populated. Empty branches must be represented as None (encoded to b'' by encode_internal_node).

reachability.notes: BranchNode construction is internal to patricialize. The 16-subnode structure is hardcoded. Violation would require a fundamental bug in the branch construction logic.

properties.severity_justification: A BranchNode with fewer or more than 16 subnodes would produce an invalid RLP encoding, causing root computation to fail or produce incorrect results.

properties.text: The _prepare_trie function must correctly convert preimage keys to Address type when calling get_storage_root. The Address(preimage) conversion must not truncate, extend, or otherwise corrupt the address bytes.

reachability.notes: If the Address(preimage) conversion is incorrect, get_storage_root would be called with the wrong address, returning the wrong storage root. This would silently corrupt the account encoding in the state trie.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect address conversion would cause the wrong storage root to be embedded in account encodings, silently corrupting the state root for affected accounts.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: PoC must demonstrate an address preimage that is incorrectly converted, causing the wrong storage root to be used.
